description:
You are an edge-computing engineer validating logs that arrive from a fleet of IoT sensors.  
    A raw, mixed-severity log already exists at  
        /home/user/deployment/logs/raw_device.log  

    Your goal is to isolate every line that simultaneously satisfies BOTH of the following conditions:  

        1. The severity tag is exactly “[ERROR]” (including the square brackets).  
        2. The DeviceID field is exactly “alpha321” (case-sensitive).  
           Every matching line in the source has the form “… DeviceID=alpha321 …” with **no quotes**.  

    What you must deliver
    ---------------------
    1. Create the directory  
           /home/user/deployment/processed  
       if it does not already exist.  

    2. Create a file named  
           /home/user/deployment/processed/alpha321_errors.log  
       that contains ONLY the original, unmodified log lines that match the two conditions above.  
       • Preserve the order in which the lines appear in the source file.  
       • Do NOT add blank lines, headers, or footers.  
       • File must end with a single trailing newline character.  

    3. Create a second file named  
           /home/user/deployment/processed/alpha321_errors_summary.log  
       with exactly ONE line whose format is  

           alpha321 total_errors:<N>  

       where <N> is the number of lines you wrote to alpha321_errors.log.  
       • No leading or trailing spaces.  
       • A final newline **is required**.  

    4. You may use any standard shell utilities (grep, egrep, awk, sed, wc, etc.), but you must meet the output-format requirements above.  

    Verification hints (for you, not for the agent)
    -----------------------------------------------
    • Automated tests will open BOTH files and compare them byte-for-byte with expected content.  
    • The summary file must contain exactly one colon character and no other punctuation.  
    • The path and filenames are case-sensitive.  
    • The agent does NOT need root privileges and should run entirely as the regular user.
truth:
<!-- Existing file before the task starts -->
    <file path="/home/user/deployment/logs/raw_device.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-08-12 14:03:22] [INFO]  DeviceID=alpha321 Temperature=23.4C
[2023-08-12 14:04:03] [WARN]  DeviceID=beta654  Battery=15%
[2023-08-12 14:05:10] [ERROR] DeviceID=alpha321 SensorFailure=Temp
[2023-08-12 14:05:46] [ERROR] DeviceID=gamma999 ConnectionLost
[2023-08-12 14:06:22] [INFO]  DeviceID=alpha321 Reconnected
[2023-08-12 14:07:00] [ERROR] DeviceID=alpha321 SensorFailure=Humidity
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected artefacts after correct task execution -->
    <file path="/home/user/deployment/processed/alpha321_errors.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-08-12 14:05:10] [ERROR] DeviceID=alpha321 SensorFailure=Temp
[2023-08-12 14:07:00] [ERROR] DeviceID=alpha321 SensorFailure=Humidity
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/deployment/processed/alpha321_errors_summary.log"><![CDATA[
alpha321 total_errors:2
]]></file>
name: task_000000_a12eb539
